description: CLI interface for Google Workspace APIs

analytics.usage_instructions: Google Analytics GA4 data access -- list accounts and properties, run custom reports, show top pages, inspect traffic sources, and view realtime metrics.

accounts.usage_instructions: List GA4 accounts and properties. Use --limit/-l to limit accounts, --table/-t for formatted output, --filter/-f for field filters, --properties/-p to choose output fields, and --profile for a named Google profile.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of accounts to list

properties.usage_instructions: List GA4 properties. Run `google analytics properties --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of properties to list

realtime.help: Show real-time active users and pages.
realtime.usage_instructions: Show real-time active users and pages. Use --property for a GA4 property ID, --table/-t for formatted output, and --profile for a named Google profile.

report.help: Run a custom GA4 report with dimensions and metrics.
report.usage_instructions: Run a custom GA4 report. --metrics/-m is required. Use --dimensions/-d, --start/-s, --end/-e, --limit/-l, --order-by, --desc/--asc, --filter/-f, --property, --table/-t, and --profile as needed. If --property is omitted, GOOGLE_ANALYTICS_PROPERTY_ID must be configured.

options.name: --order-by
options.help: Metric or dimension to order by

top-pages.usage_instructions: Show top pages by pageviews. Use --days/-d for the lookback window, --limit/-l for result count, --property for a GA4 property ID, --table/-t for formatted output, and --profile for a named Google profile.

options.name: --days
options.help: Number of days to look back

traffic.usage_instructions: Show traffic sources by session source and medium. Use --days/-d for the lookback window, --limit/-l for result count, --property for a GA4 property ID, --table/-t for formatted output, and --profile for a named Google profile.

options.name: --days
options.help: Number of days to look back

auth.usage_instructions: OAuth2 authentication for Google APIs. Use `auth login` to authenticate, `auth status` to verify, `auth logout` to clear tokens.

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.
login.usage_instructions: Authenticate with Google OAuth2. Use --force/-F to re-authenticate. Optionally provide --oauth-client-id and --oauth-client-secret for custom OAuth apps.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

logout.help: Clear stored credentials and browser sessions.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

profiles.usage_instructions: Manage authentication profiles Available subcommands: create, delete, get, list, set-default.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Create a new profile from .env.example template. Run `google auth profiles create --help` for exact arguments and options.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Delete a profile and its data. Run `google auth profiles delete --help` for exact arguments and options.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.
get.usage_instructions: Get details for a specific profile. Run `google auth profiles get --help` for exact arguments and options.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.
list.usage_instructions: List all profiles and show which is the default. Run `google auth profiles list --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

remove.help: Delete a profile and its data.

select.help: Activate a profile within its auth type.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to activate within its auth type

status.usage_instructions: Check if Google authentication is active and valid. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles. Run `google auth test --help` for exact arguments and options.

cache.usage_instructions: Manage response cache Available subcommands: clear.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed. Run `google cache clear --help` for exact arguments and options.

calendar.usage_instructions: Google Calendar event access -- list, search, and view events.

get.usage_instructions: Get a specific calendar event by EVENT_ID (positional). Use --calendar-id/-c for non-primary calendars. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

list.usage_instructions: List calendar events. Use --calendar-id/-c for a specific calendar (default: primary). Use --max-results/-l to limit results. Use --time-min and --time-max for date ranges (ISO 8601 format). Use --table/-t for formatted output.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of events to list

options.name: --days
options.help: Number of days to look ahead

search.usage_instructions: Search calendar events by keyword. QUERY is required (positional). Use --calendar-id/-c, --time-min, --time-max for scoping. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

today.usage_instructions: Show today's events. Convenience command equivalent to list with today's date range. Use --calendar-id/-c, --table/-t.

chat.usage_instructions: Access Google Chat messages Available subcommands: messages, send, spaces.

messages.usage_instructions: Manage Chat messages Available subcommands: get, list.

get.usage_instructions: Get a specific Chat message. Run `google chat messages get --help` for exact arguments and options.

list.help: List messages in a Chat space.
list.usage_instructions: List messages in a Chat space. Run `google chat messages list --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of messages to list

send.help: Send a message to a Chat space. By default, shows a preview of the message without sending. Use --confirm to actually send the message.
send.usage_instructions: Send a message to a Chat space. By default, shows a preview of the message without sending. Use --confirm to actually send the message. Run `google chat send --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.name: --confirm
options.help: Actually send the message. Without this flag, only previews

spaces.usage_instructions: Manage Chat spaces Available subcommands: get, list.

get.help: Get details of a specific Chat space.
get.usage_instructions: Get details of a specific Chat space. Run `google chat spaces get --help` for exact arguments and options.

list.help: List Chat spaces the user belongs to.
list.usage_instructions: List Chat spaces the user belongs to. Run `google chat spaces list --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of spaces to list

cloud.usage_instructions: Google Cloud resource management -- projects and credentials.

credentials.help: Manage project credentials (service accounts, API keys, OAuth clients)
credentials.usage_instructions: Nested group for service account credential management.

get.usage_instructions: Get a project credential. Run `google cloud credentials get --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of credentials to list

arguments.name: credential_id
arguments.help: Credential identifier (email, key name, or OAuth client name)

projects.usage_instructions: Nested group for GCP project operations.

create.help: Create a new Google Cloud project.
create.usage_instructions: Create a new Google Cloud project.

get.help: Get a Google Cloud project by ID.
get.usage_instructions: Get a Google Cloud project by ID.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of projects to list

services.usage_instructions: Manage Google Cloud API services Available subcommands: disable, enable, get, list.

disable.help: Disable an API service for a project.
disable.usage_instructions: Disable an API service for a project. Run `google cloud services disable --help` for exact arguments and options.

enable.help: Enable an API service for a project.
enable.usage_instructions: Enable an API service for a project. Run `google cloud services enable --help` for exact arguments and options.

get.help: Get details of an API service for a project.
get.usage_instructions: Get details of an API service for a project. Run `google cloud services get --help` for exact arguments and options.

list.help: List API services for a project.
list.usage_instructions: List API services for a project. Run `google cloud services list --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.name: --state
options.help: Filter by state: ENABLED, DISABLED, or ALL

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of services to list

docs.usage_instructions: Google Docs document management -- create, read, update, export, and manage tables.

create.usage_instructions: Create a new Google Doc. Use --title/-t for the document title. Use --content/-c for initial text content. Use --folder-id to place in a specific Drive folder.

export.help: Export a Google Doc to various formats. Supported formats: - txt: Plain text - md: Markdown (preserves headings, bold, italic, links, tables) - pdf: PDF document - docx: Microsoft Word - html: HTML - rtf: Rich Text Format - epub: EPUB ebook
examples: google docs export DOC_ID --format pdf -o document.pdf
google docs export DOC_ID --format txt > content.txt
export.usage_instructions: Export a Google Doc to a file. DOCUMENT_ID is required (positional). Use --format/-f (pdf, docx, txt, html, etc.) and --output/-o for output path.

options.name: --format
options.help: Export format: txt, pdf, docx, html, rtf, epub, md

options.name: --output
options.help: Output file path (default: stdout for text formats)

get.usage_instructions: Get document metadata by DOCUMENT_ID (positional). Use --table/-t for formatted output.

list.usage_instructions: List Google Docs. Use --folder-id to scope to a Drive folder. Use --limit/-l, --table/-t, --properties/-p.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of documents to list

read.help: Read document content (text only). Extracts 100% of all text from the document including paragraphs, tables, headers, footers, and all other content.
read.usage_instructions: Read the text content of a Google Doc by DOCUMENT_ID (positional). Returns the document body text.

tables.usage_instructions: Nested group for managing tables within Google Docs.

update.help: Update table cells in a Google Doc. Supports two modes: 1. Single cell update: Use --table, --row (or --label), --col, and --content 2. Batch updates: Use --data or --file with array of updates For batch updates, JSON format: [ {"table": 0, "row": 1, "col": 1, "content": "New value"}, {"table": 0, "label": "Course Title", "col": 1, "content": "My Course"} ] When using --label, it finds the row where the first column matches the label.
examples: google docs tables update DOC_ID --table 0 --row 1 --col 1 --content "New value"
google docs tables update DOC_ID --table 0 --label "Course Title" --col 1 --content "My Course"
update.usage_instructions: Update table cells in a Google Doc. Supports two modes: 1. Single cell update: Use --table, --row (or --label), --col, and --content 2. Batch updates: Use --data or --file with array of updates For batch updates, JSON format: [ {"table": 0, "row": 1, "col": 1, "content": "New value"}, {"table": 0, "label": "Course Title", "col": 1, "content": "My Course"} ] When using --label, it finds the row where the first column matches the label.

options.name: --data
options.help: JSON array of updates: [{table, row, col, content}, ...]

options.name: --file
options.help: Path to JSON file with updates

options.name: --label
options.help: Find row by label in first column (alternative to --row)

options.name: --list
options.help: List all tables and their structure

update.help: Update a Google Doc's content. Replaces the entire document body with new content, or appends to the end.
examples: google docs update DOC_ID --content "New content here"
update.usage_instructions: Update a Google Doc. DOCUMENT_ID is required (positional). Use --title for renaming. Use --content/-c to replace body text. Use --append/-a to add text at the end.

options.name: --content
options.help: New content to replace document body

options.name: --file
options.help: Path to file containing new content

drive.usage_instructions: Google Drive file management -- list, search, get details, and download files.

download.help: Download a file from Google Drive.
download.usage_instructions: Download a file from Drive. FILE_ID is required (positional). Use --output/-o for local path.

get.usage_instructions: Get file metadata by FILE_ID (positional). Use --table/-t for formatted output.

list.usage_instructions: List files in Google Drive. Use --folder-id to scope to a folder. Use --mime-type to filter by type. Use --limit/-l, --table/-t, --properties/-p.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of files to list

search.help: Search for files in Google Drive.
search.usage_instructions: Search Drive files by keyword. QUERY is required (positional). Use --mime-type, --limit/-l, --table/-t.

gmail.usage_instructions: Gmail message management -- list, read, search, send, reply, archive, and manage labels.

archive.help: Archive messages by removing them from inbox.
archive.usage_instructions: Archive a message by MESSAGE_ID (positional). Removes INBOX label.

download-attachment.help: Download attachment(s) from a message. Specify either --filename, --attachment-id, or --all to select which attachment(s) to download.
download-attachment.usage_instructions: Download an attachment. MESSAGE_ID and ATTACHMENT_ID required (positional). Use --output/-o for save path.

options.name: --output
options.help: Output path (defaults to current directory with original filename)

options.name: --all
options.help: Download all attachments from the message

draft.help: Create an email draft with optional attachments.
draft.usage_instructions: Create a draft email. Same options as send (--to, --subject/-s, --body/-b, etc.).

options.name: --attach
options.help: File(s) to attach (can be used multiple times)

options.name: --query
options.help: Gmail search query the message must match

options.name: --negated-query
options.help: Gmail search query the message must NOT match

options.name: --remove-label
options.help: Label ID to remove, e.g. INBOX, UNREAD, SPAM (repeatable)

options.name: --forward
options.help: Forward matching messages to this verified address

get.help: Get a Gmail filter by ID.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of filters to list

get.usage_instructions: Get message metadata by MESSAGE_ID (positional). Use --table/-t for formatted output.

options.name: --raw
options.help: Return raw API response without processing

labels.usage_instructions: Nested group for Gmail label management.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of labels to list

list.usage_instructions: List Gmail messages. Use --label/-L to filter by label (default: INBOX). Use --limit/-l, --table/-t, --properties/-p.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of messages to list

read.usage_instructions: Read full message content by MESSAGE_ID (positional). Returns headers and body text.

reply.help: Reply to a message (sender only). By default, shows a preview of the reply without sending. Use --confirm to actually send the reply.
reply.usage_instructions: Reply to a message. MESSAGE_ID is required (positional). Use --body/-b for reply text.

options.name: --attach
options.help: File(s) to attach (can be used multiple times)

options.name: --confirm
options.help: Actually send the reply. Without this flag, only previews the message

reply-all.help: Reply to all recipients of a message. By default, shows a preview of the reply without sending. Use --confirm to actually send the reply.
reply-all.usage_instructions: Reply-all to a message. MESSAGE_ID is required (positional). Use --body/-b for reply text.

options.name: --attach
options.help: File(s) to attach (can be used multiple times)

options.name: --confirm
options.help: Actually send the reply. Without this flag, only previews the message

search.usage_instructions: Search Gmail with Gmail query syntax. QUERY is required (positional). Use --limit/-l, --table/-t.

send.help: Send an email message with optional attachments. By default, shows a preview of the message without sending. Use --confirm to actually send the email.
send.usage_instructions: Send an email. Use --to (required), --subject/-s, --body/-b. Use --cc, --bcc for additional recipients. Use --attachment/-a for file attachments.

options.name: --attach
options.help: File(s) to attach (can be used multiple times)

options.name: --confirm
options.help: Actually send the email. Without this flag, only previews the message

send-draft.help: Send an existing Gmail draft. By default, prints the draft metadata without sending. Use --confirm to actually send the draft.
send-draft.usage_instructions: Send an existing Gmail draft. By default, prints the draft metadata without sending. Use --confirm to actually send the draft. Run `google gmail send-draft --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.name: --confirm
options.help: Actually send the draft. Without this flag, only previews the draft

lookerstudio.usage_instructions: Manage Looker Studio assets. Use reports list/get for REPORT assets and reports create-link/update-link for Linking API report creation URLs.

reports.usage_instructions: Work with Looker Studio REPORT assets. Use list/get for REST asset lookup, create-link/update-link for report creation URLs, and permissions-* commands for sharing operations.

create-link.help: Create a Looker Studio Linking API URL for a report.
create-link.usage_instructions: Generate a Looker Studio Linking API URL from a report ID. Use --data-source alias.key=value for generic parameters or --ga-* options for Google Analytics data sources.

options.name: --measurement-id
options.help: Google Analytics measurement ID for report usage

options.name: --keep-measurement-id
options.help: Use the template report measurement ID

options.name: --ga-refresh-fields
options.help: Whether to refresh fields: true or false

get.help: Get a Looker Studio report asset.
get.usage_instructions: Get one Looker Studio REPORT asset by report ID. Use --properties to limit returned fields and --table for table output.

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated properties to include in output

list.usage_instructions: List Looker Studio REPORT assets. Use --title, --owner, --include-trashed, --order-by, --limit, --filter, and --properties to narrow output.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of reports to list

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated properties to include in output

permissions-add-members.help: [OPTIONS] REPORT_ID Add members to a Looker Studio report permission role.
permissions-add-members.usage_instructions: Add members to a Looker Studio report permission role by report ID.

permissions-get.help: Get permissions for a Looker Studio report.
permissions-get.usage_instructions: Get permissions for a Looker Studio report by report ID.

permissions-patch.help: Patch permissions for a Looker Studio report.
permissions-patch.usage_instructions: Patch permissions for a Looker Studio report by report ID using --permissions-json.

permissions-revoke-all.help: Revoke all permissions for members on a Looker Studio report.
permissions-revoke-all.usage_instructions: Revoke all permissions for members on a Looker Studio report by report ID.

update-link.help: Create a Linking API URL with data-source params.
update-link.usage_instructions: Generate a Looker Studio Linking API URL that updates data source parameters when a new report is created from the report ID. This does not mutate the source report.

options.name: --measurement-id
options.help: Google Analytics measurement ID for report usage

options.name: --keep-measurement-id
options.help: Use the template report measurement ID

options.name: --ga-refresh-fields
options.help: Whether to refresh fields: true or false

searchconsole.usage_instructions: Google Search Console access -- manage sites, URL inspection, and index status.

index.help: Request Google to crawl and index a URL via URL Inspection API.

options.name: --site-url
options.help: Search Console site URL (overrides GOOGLE_SEARCHCONSOLE_SITE env var)

get.help: Get details for a specific Search Console site.
get.usage_instructions: Get details for a specific Search Console site.

arguments.name: site_url
arguments.help: Site URL to get details for

options.name: --table
options.help: Output as table instead of JSON

options.name: --table
options.help: Output as table instead of JSON

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of sites to return

urls.help: Query indexed URLs and search analytics

get.help: Get indexing status and details for a specific URL. Uses the URL Inspection API to retrieve indexing information including: - Index status and verdict - Crawl information - Mobile usability - Rich results status
get.usage_instructions: Get indexing status and details for a specific URL. Uses the URL Inspection API to retrieve indexing information including: - Index status and verdict - Crawl information - Mobile usability - Rich results status

options.name: --site-url
options.help: Search Console site URL (overrides GOOGLE_SEARCHCONSOLE_SITE env var)

options.name: --table
options.help: Output as table instead of JSON

list.help: List indexed URLs with search performance data. Returns URLs that have appeared in search results within the date range, along with clicks, impressions, CTR, and average position.
list.usage_instructions: List indexed URLs with search performance data. Returns URLs that have appeared in search results within the date range, along with clicks, impressions, CTR, and average position.

options.name: --site-url
options.help: Search Console site URL (overrides GOOGLE_SEARCHCONSOLE_SITE env var)

options.name: --start-date
options.help: Start date in YYYY-MM-DD format (default: 30 days ago)

options.name: --end-date
options.help: End date in YYYY-MM-DD format (default: today)

options.name: --type
options.help: Search type: web, image, video, discover, googleNews, news

options.name: --table
options.help: Output as table instead of JSON

options.name: --limit

sheets.usage_instructions: Google Sheets spreadsheet management -- create, read, update, and append data.

append.usage_instructions: Append rows to a sheet. SPREADSHEET_ID is required (positional). Use --range/-r and --values/-v (JSON array of arrays).

create.usage_instructions: Create a new spreadsheet. Use --title/-t for the name. Use --sheet-name for the initial sheet tab name.

get.usage_instructions: Get spreadsheet metadata by SPREADSHEET_ID (positional). Use --table/-t for formatted output.

list.usage_instructions: List Google Sheets. Use --folder-id to scope to a folder. Use --limit/-l, --table/-t, --properties/-p.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of spreadsheets to list

read.usage_instructions: Read cell data from a sheet. SPREADSHEET_ID is required (positional). Use --range/-r (e.g., 'Sheet1!A1:D10'). Use --table/-t for formatted output.

update.help: Update values in specific cells or range.
update.usage_instructions: Update cells in a sheet. SPREADSHEET_ID is required (positional). Use --range/-r and --values/-v (JSON array of arrays).

options.name: --range
options.help: Cell or range to update (e.g., Sheet1!A1, Sheet1!A1:C3)

options.name: --values
options.help: Values to write (comma-separated for row, semicolon-separated for multiple rows)

options.name: --raw
options.help: Use RAW input (default is USER_ENTERED which parses formulas/numbers)

webstore.usage_instructions: Manage Chrome Web Store items Available subcommands: cancel-submission, get, list, listing, package, publish, release, rollout, status, upload, upload-extension.

cancel-submission.help: Cancel the active Chrome Web Store submission for an item.
cancel-submission.usage_instructions: Cancel the active Chrome Web Store submission for an item. Run `google webstore cancel-submission --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.help: Chrome Web Store publisher ID; defaults to CWS_PUBLISHER_ID

options.name: --item-id
options.help: Chrome Web Store item or extension ID; defaults to CWS_EXTENSION_ID

list.help: List the configured Chrome Web Store item status.

options.name: --item-id

status.usage_instructions: Fetch Chrome Web Store item status. Run `google webstore status --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.name: --item-id
options.help: Chrome Web Store item or extension ID; defaults to CWS_EXTENSION_ID

upload.help: Upload a package to an existing Chrome Web Store item.
upload.usage_instructions: Upload a package to an existing Chrome Web Store item. Run `google webstore upload --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.name: --item-id

options.help: Command to run before packaging; repeat for multiple commands

options.name: --exclude
options.help: Additional package-relative glob to exclude; repeat for multiple patterns

usage_instructions: Use the google CLI to access Google services. Run `google auth login` before API-backed commands, and use `--profile` when working with a non-default profile.
